commands.review_summary:
The PR adds container-aware EACCES recovery hints to `openclaw update` result output and covers host/container hint behavior in focused tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +12. Total +23 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main routes npm global update/install EACCES failures to host-oriented recovery hints, and the PR adds a directly testable container branch in that same helper. I did not run a live container update failure in this read-only review.

target.title: fix: guide container operators to redeploy after update permission failures
target.body:
Related: #94279

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running `openclaw update` directly inside a
container could receive host-oriented EACCES recovery guidance such as using
`sudo` or changing the npm global prefix.

Those steps are misleading inside a container because an in-container package
update is not durable across recreation.

## Why This Change Was Made

When a global update or install step fails with EACCES in a detected container,
the updater now recommends pulling or building the target OpenClaw image and
recreating or redeploying the container. Existing host recovery guidance is
preserved outside containers.

This complements #94310, which suppresses container update suggestions and
actions. It covers direct CLI invocation of `openclaw update`.

## User Impact

Container operators who invoke the updater directly now receive recovery steps
that match the durable container deployment model instead of host package
installation advice.

## Evidence

- Added a container-specific EACCES regression test that requires image
  redeploy guidance and rejects `sudo` and npm-prefix advice.
- Existing host EACCES tests explicitly prove that host recovery guidance is
  unchanged.
- Passed:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts`
  - focused `oxfmt --check`
  - `git diff --check`
  - changed-lane dry run (`core`, `coreTests`)
- Codex autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Broad pre-push runner validation was unavailable because remote runner
  allocation failed before the check executed; PR CI remains the broad gate.
